name: Time Based Cover
description: The `time_based` cover platform allows you to create covers with position control that do not have any position feedback. The state of the cover is thus always an assumed one, the current position is approximated with the time the cover has been moving in a direction. The state of the cover can be restored at node reboot.

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name for the cover. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the cover in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Device Class
config_entries.description: The device class for the sensor. See [https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/cover/#device-class](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/cover/#device-class) for a list of available options.

config_entries.label: Close Action
config_entries.description: The action that should be performed when the remote requests the cover to be closed.

config_entries.label: Close Duration
config_entries.description: The amount of time it takes the cover to close from the fully-open state.

config_entries.label: Open Action
config_entries.description: The action that should be performed when the remote requests the cover to be opened.

config_entries.label: Open Duration
config_entries.description: The amount of time it takes the cover to open up from the fully-closed state.

config_entries.label: Stop Action
config_entries.description: The action that should be performed to stop the cover when the remote requests the cover to be stopped or when the cover has been opening/closing for the given durations.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Assumed State
config_entries.description: Whether the true state of the cover is not known. This will make the Home Assistant frontend show buttons for both OPEN and CLOSE actions, instead of hiding or disabling one of them. Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Has Built In Endstop
config_entries.description: Indicates that the cover has built in end stop detectors. In this configuration the `stop_action` is not performed when the open or close time is completed and if the cover is commanded to open or close the corresponding actions will be performed without checking current state. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: Manual Control
config_entries.description: For covers with manual external controls. With this configuration if the cover is commanded to open or close the corresponding actions will be performed even if the current state fully open or fully closed matches desired state, then `stop_action` will be called after the full duration of the action elapses. The current state will then be relearned upon completion. It's recommended to set `assumed_state` to true so the cover control button aren't disabled in the interface. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: MQTT Json State Payload
config_entries.description: When set to `true`, state changes will be published only to the `state_topic` as a single JSON object per state change. Example: ```json { "state": "open", "position": 100, "tilt": 50 } ``` When `false`, individual values are published to the `state_topic`, `position_state_topic`, and `tilt_state_topic` separately. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Position Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive cover position commands on.

config_entries.label: Position State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish cover position changes to. Not valid if `mqtt_json_state_payload` is set to `true`.

config_entries.label: Tilt Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive cover tilt commands on.

config_entries.label: Tilt State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish cover cover tilt state changes to. Not valid if `mqtt_json_state_payload` is set to `true`.
